name: Cafe Cappuccino
directions: Stir ingredients together.
Process in a blender until powdered.
Use 2 Tbsp.  of mixture for each cup of hot water.

similar.name: Easy Mocha / Mochaccino (De-caf is possible if wanted)

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients and store in an air-tight container.
This recipe will yield approximately 8 pint size gift jars.
Attach the following directions on a card: Combine 3 heaping tablespoons of cocoa mix in hot water or milk.

name: No Sugar Added Iced Coffee
directions: Combine all the ingredients in food processor or blender.
Blend until smooth.
Top with a bit of whipped cream if you want to indulge.

name: Iced Coffee (Dunkin Donuts)
directions: Put sugar in a large glass.
Drop in ice cubes.
Pour coffee over ice and add milk.
Stir until sugar is melted.
That's it!

similar.name: Easy Mocha / Mochaccino (De-caf is possible if wanted)

name: Lite Chocolate Cola
directions: In a glass combine the chocolate syrup and the cola, stir well.
Add ice cubes.

similar.name: Easy Mocha / Mochaccino (De-caf is possible if wanted)

name: Yahoolie Chocolate Drink
directions: In a gallon container, mix nestle powder and sugar with lukewarm water.
Stir until sugar is dissolved.
Add "creamer", vanilla and salt.
Mix and add cold water or water and ice to fill the container.
Serve cold.
Stir before serving because the chocolate settles to the bottom of the container.

name: Easy Mocha / Mochaccino (De-caf is possible if wanted)
directions: Put in hot chocolate powder& cappuccino powder (if wanted) (if you are using coffee powder, put this in now.) Put in hot water and stir till dissolved.
Put in"Folgers instant coffee single" (if used), and dunk until flavor is reached.
Take out instant coffee single and serve.

name: After Dinner Coffee
directions: Place brandy and coffee liqueur in cup.
Fill with black coffee.
Top with dollop of cream.

similar.name: Easy Mocha / Mochaccino (De-caf is possible if wanted)

name: Chocolate Soda
directions: In a tall glass, combine the sugar and the cocoa.
Add the milk and stir until well blended.
Slowly pour in the seltzer, pausing to allow some bubbles to disappear to prevent overflowing the glass.
Serve immediately with long handled spoon and a straw.
